assertion.text: Erdős Problem #969: declared status 'open'. Formalized: no. Let $Q(x)$ count the number of squarefree integers in $[1,x]$. Determine the order of magnitude in the error term in the asymptotic\[Q(x)=\frac{6}{\pi^2}x+E(x).\] Current best: The best known unconditional upper bound is of the shape $x^{1/2-o(1)}$, due to Walfisz \cite{Wa63}. Conditional on this assumption, the best known upper bound is\[E(x)\ll x^{\frac{11}{35}+o(1)},\]due to Liu \cite{Li16}. Prize: no. OEIS: A013928. Tags: number theory.
